oracle_clarifying_responses.clarification_response: Historically and religiously, Sunday became the seventh day in ancient Jewish tradition based on the creation narrative in Genesis, where God rested on the seventh day (Saturday), but early Christians began observing "the Lord's Day" (Sunday) as the first day of the week; however, if referring to the Sabbath specifically, that is Saturday. oracle_clarifying_responses.clarification_response: Under ISO 8601, Sunday became the first day of the week (not the seventh) in 1988 when the standard was officially adopted internationally; prior to that, Monday was traditionally considered the first day in many modern contexts, though Sunday remained the first day in religious and some cultural calendars. Note: ISO 8601 defines Monday as Day 1 and Sunday as Day 7, so if you are asking when Sunday became Day 7 under this standard, it has been that way since the standard's adoption in 1988.
